title: Dutiva generated guidance card
description: Canonical schema for guidance cards emitted by advisor-training/pipelines/build-guidance-layer.ts. These cards are the output of the source-law ingestion pipeline and the input to the runtime retrieval layer via data/advisor-guidance-index.json. Do NOT confuse this with advisor-training/metadata/curated-review-card.schema.json, which is for the future human-curated card review layer.

id.description: Stable SHA-1-derived 24-character hex ID for this guidance card.

source_normalized_id.description: ID of the normalized provision record that produced this card.

source_record_id.description: ID of the original parsed provision record.

source_file.description: Relative path to the source normalized JSON file.

guidance_version.description: SemVer of the guidance layer that produced this card.

jurisdiction.description: Human-readable jurisdiction label, e.g. 'Canada (Federal)'. For runtime use, the adapter maps this to the engine code FEDERAL, ON, QC, etc.

language.description: Language of the source provision. 'unknown' triggers a quality_warning.

topics.description: All topic classifications that scored above zero for this provision.

risk_level.description: Risk level derived from topic classification. High-risk topics include Termination, Harassment, Accommodation, Workplace Safety.

applies_to.description: Inferred roles that this provision applies to.

law_title.description: Short title of the source law, e.g. 'Canada Labour Code'. Must prefer short title over long descriptive title.

citation.description: Full citation string, e.g. 'Canada Labour Code, s. 230(1)'. Must not be a bare subsection like 's. (a)' or 's. (3)' without parent section context.

status.description: Whether this provision is active or has been repealed/inactive. Runtime loaders must exclude inactive_or_repealed records by default.

user_questions.description: Template HR questions this card answers. Drawn from QUESTION_TEMPLATES by topic.

advisor_answer_en.description: English advisor answer. This is the primary runtime content field. Must be plain prose — no raw Markdown bold/italic/headings/bullet hyphens.

advisor_answer_fr_placeholder.description: French placeholder text. NOT validated and NOT user-facing. Must never be exposed in public responses. Replaced by validated French text in the future bilingual layer.

legal_basis.description: Ordered list of citation strings for this card's legal basis. Typically [citation, law_title].

search_text.description: Combined search text for keyword retrieval. Maps to the runtime GuidanceItem.search_text field.

xml_path.description: XPath-like path to the source node in the original XML.

quality_warnings.description: Quality warnings from parsing and normalization. The runtime adapter maps this to GuidanceItem.qualityWarnings.

embedding_id.description: Optional. Stable 24-char hex ID for the embedding record. Added by build-guidance-index-keyword.ts or build-guidance-embeddings.ts when the card is enriched into the guidance index.

embedding_text.description: Optional. Text used for embedding/indexing this card. Added by the index pipeline. Not part of the base guidance layer output.

embedding.description: Optional. Vector embedding of the card's embedding_text. Added by build-guidance-embeddings.ts (local Xenova variant) or build-guidance-embeddings-hf.ts (HF Inference API). Absent in keyword-only indexes.

examples.advisor_answer_en: Moderate-risk issue. Review the legal source, document your steps, and apply the rule consistently. Topic: Leave. Based on Canada Labour Code, s. 230(1), the relevant rule or source text indicates: ...
examples.advisor_answer_fr_placeholder: Vérifiez les faits, la juridiction applicable et les documents internes avant d'agir. Sujet : Leave. Source : Canada Labour Code, s. 230(1). Résumé français complet à générer lors de la couche bilingue validée.

guardrails.disclaimer_en: This is general HR compliance guidance, not legal advice. For high-risk matters, consult a qualified employment lawyer or HR professional.
guardrails.disclaimer_fr: Il s'agit de renseignements généraux en conformité RH, et non de conseils juridiques. Pour les situations à risque élevé, consultez un avocat en droit de l'emploi ou un professionnel RH qualifié.

retrieval.search_text: Leave Canada Labour Code Canada Labour Code, s. 230(1) ...
